title: Directions & Places
shortSummary: Ask for and understand directions, and name the places around a German town.
culturalNote: German towns are built around a central Marktplatz (market square) and are very walkable and cyclable, with excellent public transport. People generally give precise, accurate directions and will often walk you part of the way. Knowing the difference between the U-Bahn (underground), S-Bahn (suburban rail), and Straßenbahn (tram) helps a lot in any city.

keyPhrases.english: How do I get to the station?
to the left / to the right

grammar.explanation: To say 'to the' for a destination, German contracts: zu + dem = zum (masculine/neuter) and zu + der = zur (feminine). 'Wie komme ich zum Markt?' but 'zur Post'.

examples.en: How do I get to the station? (masculine)
How do I get to the pharmacy? (feminine)

grammar.commonMistake: Using zum for feminine places: it is zur Apotheke, not zum Apotheke.

grammar.explanation: Directions often come as polite commands with Sie: take the verb, add the subject Sie. 'Gehen Sie geradeaus' (Go straight), 'Nehmen Sie die erste Straße links'.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting to include Sie in the formal command: it is 'Gehen Sie', not just 'Gehen'.

dialogue.setup: A tourist stops a passer-by near the town square to find the train station.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.german: Entschuldigung, wie komme ich zum Bahnhof?
lines.english: Excuse me, how do I get to the station?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.german: Gehen Sie geradeaus bis zur Ampel.
lines.english: Go straight ahead to the traffic light.

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.german: Dann biegen Sie links ab. Der Bahnhof ist gegenüber der Post.
lines.english: Then turn left. The station is opposite the post office.

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.german: Nein, nur fünf Minuten zu Fuß.
lines.english: No, just five minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.english: Thank you very much for your help!

quiz.question: How do you say 'straight ahead'?
quiz.explanation: geradeaus is the single word for 'straight ahead'.

quiz.question: Which is correct for a feminine destination (die Post)?
quiz.explanation: zu + der = zur for feminine nouns.

quiz.question: Gehen _____ geradeaus. (formal command)
quiz.explanation: The formal imperative keeps Sie after the verb.

quiz.question: Arrange: komme / wie / zum / ich / Bahnhof
quiz.explanation: Question word wie, verb komme second, then ich, then the destination.
